title: eriogonum douglasii var. elkoense
intro: Eriogonum douglasii var. elkoense, also known as Elko buckwheat or Elko desert buckwheat, is a shrubby plant belonging to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). The plant's medicinal properties have been used by Native American tribes for various purposes, including as a treatment for wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum douglasii var. elkoense include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
